question: Why is the Double V Campaign significant to a study of WWII?

options.A: It highlights the contradiction of fighting fascism abroad amid racism at home and prefigured the Civil Rights Movement
options.B: It ended segregation in the army immediately
options.C: It was a Nazi recruitment tool
options.D: It had no connection to civil rights

explanation: It exposed Allied hypocrisy on race and accelerated postwar challenges to racial hierarchy.
